# services.duplicity

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.duplicity`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.duplicity.cleanup.maxAge` | `null or string` | If non-null, delete all backup sets older than the given time. Old backup sets will not be deleted if backup sets newer than time depend on them. |
| `services.duplicity.cleanup.maxFull` | `null or signed integer` | If non-null, delete all backups sets that are older than the count:th last full backup (in other words, keep the last count full backups and associated incremental sets). |
| `services.duplicity.cleanup.maxIncr` | `null or signed integer` | If non-null, delete incremental sets of all backups sets that are older than the count:th last full backup (in other words, keep only old full backups and not their increments). |
| `services.duplicity.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable backups with duplicity. |
| `services.duplicity.exclude` | `list of string` | List of paths to exclude from backups. See the FILE SELECTION section in {manpage}`duplicity(1)` for details on the syntax. |
| `services.duplicity.excludeFileList` | `null or absolute path` | File containing newline-separated list of paths to exclude into the backups. See the FILE SELECTION section in {manpage}`duplicity(1)` for details on the syntax. |
| `services.duplicity.extraFlags` | `list of string` | Extra command-line flags passed to duplicity. See {manpage}`duplicity(1)`. |
| `services.duplicity.frequency` | `null or string` | Run duplicity with the given frequency (see {manpage}`systemd.time(7)` for the format). If null, do not run automatically. |
| `services.duplicity.fullIfOlderThan` | `string` | If `"never"` (the default) always do incremental backups (the first backup will be a full backup, of course). If `"always"` always do full backups. Otherwise, this must be a string representing a duration. Full backups will be made when the latest full backup is older than this duration. If this is not the case, an incremental backup is performed. |
| `services.duplicity.include` | `list of string` | List of paths to include into the backups. See the FILE SELECTION section in {manpage}`duplicity(1)` for details on the syntax. |
| `services.duplicity.includeFileList` | `null or absolute path` | File containing newline-separated list of paths to include into the backups. See the FILE SELECTION section in {manpage}`duplicity(1)` for details on the syntax. |
| `services.duplicity.root` | `absolute path` | Root directory to backup. |
| `services.duplicity.secretFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path of a file containing secrets (gpg passphrase, access key...) in the format of EnvironmentFile as described by {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)`. For example: `PASSPHRASE=«...» AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=«...» AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=«...»` |
| `services.duplicity.targetUrl` | `string` | Target url to backup to. See the URL FORMAT section in {manpage}`duplicity(1)` for supported urls. |
